reasoning: The Lean statement captures all essential elements of the informal claim. `n : ℕ+` enforces n ≥ 1; `orderOf x = n` together with `closure {x} = ⊤` says G is cyclic of order n generated by x. The right-hand side of the iff (`H ≠ ⊤ ∧ ∀ K, H ≤ K → K = H ∨ K = ⊤`) is exactly the standard definition of H being a maximal subgroup (proper subgroup with no proper supergroup strictly between it and the whole group). The left-hand side (`∃ p, Prime p ∧ p ∣ n ∧ H = Subgroup.closure {x^p}`) correctly encodes 'H = ⟨x^p⟩ for some prime p dividing n'. Note that `Prime p` on ℕ coincides with `Nat.Prime p`, the coercion of n : ℕ+ to ℕ makes `p ∣ n` well-typed, and `Subgroup.closure {x^p}` is the subgroup generated by x^p. The iff direction swap is semantically neutral.
